skill.name: cc-skill-strategic-compact
skill.description: Suggests strategic context compaction checkpoints for Claude Code sessions using a lightweight Bash hook.
skill.summary: Prompts users to compact long coding sessions at planned checkpoints.
skill.author: affaan-m

security_audit.summary: Static findings were mostly false positives after review. SKILL.md does not contain weak cryptography, and the home-directory paths are comments that document hook setup. The script does execute under Bash and writes a predictable temporary counter file, which is a minor local filesystem concern but not evidence of malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Predictable Temporary Counter File
low_findings.description: The script stores its counter in /tmp using the process ID. This is a local reliability and minor filesystem safety concern because predictable temporary paths can be affected by preexisting files or symlinks on shared systems.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly defines and writes a predictable /tmp path. The impact appears low because it only stores a numeric counter and has no network or credential access.

low_findings.title: Benign Shell Execution Context
low_findings.description: The file is a Bash hook script and uses command substitution to read a local counter. No evidence found of user-controlled command execution, network calls, credential access, or hidden command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution reads a file path defined in the same script, and arithmetic is performed locally. The reviewed code shows no dynamic command strings or unsafe eval-like behavior.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alert on SKILL.md is unsupported by the file contents. The hidden home-directory alerts point to commented setup documentation, not active filesystem reads or writes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited SKILL.md line is plain metadata, and the home-directory paths are preceded by comment markers. There is no executable cryptographic or home-directory access code at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Predictable /tmp File Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The hook uses a predictable /tmp filename instead of a safer temporary-file creation pattern. This is a low-severity local filesystem pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in the script. The risk is limited because the file stores only the hook counter.

content.value_statement: Long AI coding sessions can lose useful context when compaction happens at poor times. This skill suggests planned compact checkpoints so users can preserve context between phases.
actual_capabilities: Provides a Bash script intended for Claude Code hook execution.
Tracks tool-call counts with a local temporary counter file.
Prints compact suggestions when the configured threshold is reached.
Supports a COMPACT_THRESHOLD environment variable for custom timing.
Includes commented example hook configuration for PreToolUse events.
limitations: Does not inspect project state or determine task phase semantically.
Uses a process-specific temporary counter that may not persist across hook invocations.
Requires manual installation into a compatible Claude Code hook setup.

use_cases.title: Control Long Feature Sessions
use_cases.description: Use compact reminders during multi-step implementation work so context checkpoints happen between planning, coding, and review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Hook Practices
use_cases.description: Use the sample hook pattern as a starting point for consistent context management across shared Claude Code workflows.

use_cases.title: Reduce Mid-Task Context Loss
use_cases.description: Prompt manual compaction before switching from exploration to execution in large repositories.

prompt_templates.title: Install Basic Reminder
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me install this strategic compact hook for Claude Code and explain what each configuration line does.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Compact Threshold
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my coding workflow and recommend a COMPACT_THRESHOLD value for this strategic compact script.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt Hook Timing
prompt_templates.prompt: Modify the compact reminder strategy so it triggers at phase transitions and after long tool-call sequences.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Hook Safety
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this strategic compact hook for filesystem safety, persistence behavior, and false-positive security scanner findings.

output_examples.input: A session reaches the configured tool-call threshold.
output_examples.output: The hook prints a short reminder that this may be a good time to run a manual compact command.

output_examples.input: A user wants later reminders in a long implementation session.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a higher threshold and explains the tradeoff between fewer prompts and longer retained context.

output_examples.input: A team reviews whether to publish the hook internally.
output: The review identifies the temporary counter file behavior.
The review confirms there are no network calls or credential reads.
The review recommends safer temporary-file handling before broad rollout.

best_practices: Use the reminder at phase boundaries, such as after exploration or before implementation.
Set COMPACT_THRESHOLD to match the expected length of your normal sessions.
Review hook scripts before installing them in personal or team Claude Code settings.
anti_patterns: Do not treat reminders as automatic context preservation.
Do not install hook scripts without checking their filesystem behavior.
Do not use one threshold for every project size and workflow.

faq.question: Does this skill compact context automatically?
faq.answer: No. It only prints reminders so the user can decide when to compact.

faq.question: Does it work with Codex?
faq.answer: The concept applies to Codex workflows, but the included script is written for Claude Code hook usage.

faq.question: Does it send data over the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found of network access in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Can I change when reminders appear?
faq.answer: Yes. The script reads COMPACT_THRESHOLD, which can change the first reminder count.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: The script uses a predictable temporary counter file, so safer temporary-file handling is recommended.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: It is useful for developers who run long Claude Code sessions and want planned context checkpoints.
